title: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States analyzes how incumbent Oklahoma Sen. Paul Scott lost his SD43 Republican primary runoff to Jessica Garvin, arguing that Scott's abstention on the SB13 vote drove abolitionist Kaity Keith into the race and split the vote, while also noting Garvin's stated willingness to support The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.
audiences: Pro-life legislator weighing political risk of abstention
Skeptic asking whether abolitionist activism affects elections

questions.question: What was the final vote share that ousted Paul Scott in the SD43 Republican primary runoff?
questions.answer: Challenger Jessica Garvin defeated incumbent Paul Scott by receiving 51.56 percent of the vote in the runoff.
questions.quote: In the OK SD43 Republican primary runoff, incumbent Paul Scott fell to challenger Jessica Garvin who received 51.56 percent of the vote.

questions.question: How did Paul Scott vote when SB13 came up for a procedural motion on March 11, 2020?
questions.audience: Pro-life legislator weighing political risk of abstention
questions.answer: Scott abstained on the motion to table SB13, apparently trying to avoid putting his position on either side of the abolition bill on the record.
questions.quote: When the motions were announced, Scott abstained, appearing to not want his vote on record one way or the other.

questions.question: What did Senate Floor Leader Kim David do before the SB13 motions were called?
questions.audience: Pro-life legislator weighing political risk of abstention
questions.answer: David spent more than thirty minutes walking the Senate floor lobbying Republican colleagues to support her motion to table SB13 and keep abortion legal in Oklahoma for another year.
questions.quote: Prior to either motion being announced, David roamed the Senate floor for more than half-an-hour whipping up votes among the Republican caucus (of which she is the second-ranking member) for her motion to keep abortion legal.

questions.question: What motivated Kaity Keith to enter the SD43 race against Scott?
questions.answer: Keith, a constituent of Scott's, was horrified that he refused to vote to bring SB13 out of committee and decided to run because she believed someone had to stand up for the abolition of abortion.
questions.quote: Watching the events closely was Scott's constituent Kaity Keith. Horrified by his refusal to vote to bring SB13 out of committee, she decided to throw her hat in the ring for the seat.

questions.question: How close did Paul Scott come to avoiding a runoff in the June 30 primary?
questions.answer: Scott missed an outright majority by only 14 votes, finishing at 49.9 percent with 5,685 votes while Garvin and Keith trailed at 39.3 and 10.9 percent respectively.
questions.quote: In the primary on June 30th, Paul Scott finished in first with 49.9 percent (5,685 votes). Garvin and Keith trailed with 39.3 percent (4,474 votes) and 10.9 percent (1,237 votes) respectively. Scott was a mere 14 votes away from the majority necessary to win the nomination.

questions.question: Why does the article argue that Scott's SB13 abstention is what cost him the seat?
questions.audience: Skeptic asking whether abolitionist activism affects elections
questions.answer: Without Keith in the race, Free the States contends Scott would have picked up far more than 14 of her 1,237 votes and won outright on June 30; instead the abstention drove Keith to run, forced a runoff, and Scott ultimately lost it.
questions.quote: Had Keith not joined the race, Scott, beyond any shadow of a doubt, would have received more than 14 of the 1,237 votes for Keith and won the nomination on June 30th.

questions.question: What position did Jessica Garvin take on The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act?
questions.answer: On her returned questionnaire Garvin said she would support the bill, even though she also said she would back compromise abortion legislation; Free the States said she would author or co-author the bill.
questions.quote: While Garvin said that she would support compromises with abortion, she also said that she would support The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.

questions.question: Why did Free the States endorse Kaity Keith instead of Jessica Garvin despite Garvin's stated support?
questions.answer: Free the States endorsed Keith because she was the only consistent abolitionist in the field, while still being grateful that Garvin returned a questionnaire and pledged to back the bill.
questions.quote: We endorsed Keith because she was the consistent abolitionist in the race, but we were nonetheless very appreciative to receive a questionnaire from Garvin and happy to learn that she would author or co-author the bill.

questions.question: What did Free the States plan to do with Garvin once she took office?
questions.answer: They intended to keep persuading her that compromise legislation sustains abortion and that only immediate abolition is faithful, while still working with her on the abolition bill she said she would support.
questions.quote: While we hope to persuade Garvin (as we hope to persuade everyone) that compromises with abortion are what is sustaining it, and that nothing short of the immediate abolition of abortion is required to be obedient to God, to uphold the Constitution, and to meaningfully love our preborn neighbors who are being murdered every single day that abortion isn't abolished, we are grateful for her expressed support for the bill and look forward to working with her along those lines.

questions.question: How did Keith frame her decision to run on her own campaign website?
questions.answer: Keith said she ran because only four current state senators stood for human life in March 2020, arguing one cannot claim to favor life while voting for death.
questions.quote: I decided to run for state Senate when ONLY FOUR of our current Senators stood for human life this March. We cannot claim to be in favor of life, yet vote for death.
